title: amalia majalis
intro: Amalia majalis, commonly known as the 'Black-eyed Susan', is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. It is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to improve digestion, reduce stress, and boost the immune system. The therapeutic actions of Amalia majalis include calming the nervous system, reducing anxiety and insomnia, and acting as a natural antihistamine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Amalia majalis can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive consumption leading to dizziness, nausea, and headaches. Overuse of the plant's extracts can also cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation and respiratory problems. Furthermore, Amalia majalis can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and worsen conditions like high blood pressure and diabetes. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can lead to kidney damage and liver toxicity.
